question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
note: He names a concrete blocker to next-level growth: bigger scope is the goal, but he is still operating in execution details rather than at broader scope.

question.label: Execution details
question.name: Which execution details are still taking too much of your time?
question.description: Drills into the specific tactical work keeping him below the scope he wants.

answer: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
note: He names a specific pattern: political conversations make him retreat into direct delivery, signalling a strong clarity gap about how to operate at his level.

question.label: Political conversations
question.name: In those political conversations, what feels hardest to navigate?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic that makes him retreat from senior-level influence into execution.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
note: He names a specific senior-level gap: hesitation around shaping cross-org alignment, which is a strong clarity signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] cross-org alignment hesitation worth picking up next session.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
note: He names confidence in senior forums as the stretch point, signaling a mild growth constraint and some hesitation to lean in; also reveals a clarity/trust thread around senior-forum expectations.

question.label: Senior forums
question.name: In senior forums, what makes you feel most unprepared?
question.description: Drills into the confidence thread to surface the specific gap underneath his hesitation.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
note: He names a specific self-limiting pattern — not being explicit about desired opportunities — which signals mild growth and engagement drag; this also hints at a clarity issue off-signature.

question.label: Wanted opportunities
question.name: What opportunities have you not been explicit about wanting?
question.description: Drills into the named opportunities thread to make his aspiration concrete before the arc progresses.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
note: He names a concrete growth aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is strong positive growth signal; wellbeing is mildly positive through energized forward-looking ambition.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
note: He names delegation as the mechanism to create room for bigger-scope work, which signals forward-looking growth intent; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' is a concrete thread to pick up next session.

question.label: Ownership boundary
question.name: What are you still carrying personally that should live with the team?
question.description: Tests whether he is holding onto executor work that should be delegated or systematized.

answer: A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
note: Names a missing growth contract with milestones as the absent structure he needs, which is a strong clarity deficit and mild engagement drag in how he is owning the shift.

question.label: First growth move
question.name: What is the first shift you want to make before we meet again?
question.description: Closes by turning the conversation into one concrete next move he owns.

answer: I want to own one strategic bet end-to-end and set a milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.
note: He named a specific next growth move and a concrete stakeholder milestone, indicating positive growth direction and clearer near-term focus.
